what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to a target image asset and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-assets/icons/image (25)
No body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: Uses an image:// URI scheme to reference the actual asset
Attachment id is att-1f6cab02 and is stored as a standalone markdown file in channel-memory/channel_attachments
conventions: Attachment records stored as individual markdown files named by attachment id
concerns: Target URI contains parentheses and a space, which may need careful escaping or handling
No checksum, size, or author metadata for the attachment
notes: This is a lightweight attachment ledger entry that points to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does 'image (25)' refer to — is it a filename, generated label, or index?
Is the image:// URI resolvable by the consuming system?
Who added this attachment and from what source?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it records an image attachment reference with channel, type, target, and timestamp.
intention_signals: To create a durable, queryable record associating an attachment identifier with a specific image asset and capture the moment it was registered.
contribution: Provides a concrete attachment reference that maps an attachment id to an image asset location and an addition timestamp, enabling traceability for design-assets channel content.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that records an image attachment (att-1f6cab02) targeting image://design-assets/icons/image (25) and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and persist a reference to an image asset associated with the design-assets channel.
